methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $100M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Ocean Infinity, a Growth-stage maritime technology company founded by Oliver Plunkett, operates in a sparsely populated comparable universe. The analysis draws on a single private peer—Orbital Marine Power—and no public market comps, reflecting the nascent nature of the ocean and maritime frontier-tech sector.

The valuation relies on stage-progression methodology applied to Ocean Infinity's $100M+ cumulative capital raise. Using a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical for progression from Growth stage to the next inflection point, this method implies a valuation of $120M. The low-end estimate of $102M represents a conservative floor anchored to total raised, while the high-end of $138M reflects upside scenarios under stage-progression assumptions.

The analysis yields a triangulated range of $102M to $138M, with a median of $120M. Key uncertainties stem from the limited comp set—reliance on a single private peer and absence of public market validation—and the inherent opacity of frontier-tech valuations in early-scale phases. The stage-progression method, while standard for Growth-stage companies, remains sensitive to multiplier assumptions in a sector with limited historical precedent. Further validation would benefit from expanded comparable data or revenue/EBITDA metrics once available.
